_note: This is a structural example only. All values are placeholders. Do not use as real scientific data. See docs/templates/STUDY_EXTRACTION_TEMPLATE.json for the blank template.
citation: Placeholder Author A, Placeholder Author B. Effect of [Species] extract on [Outcome] in [Population]: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial. Journal of Phytomedicine. 2024;12(3):45-58.

intervention.dose: 500 mg extract (equivalent to 2000 mg dried plant material)

population_or_model.condition: [Primary condition studied, e.g. mild cognitive impairment / chronic stress / specific disease]

primary: [Primary outcome measure, e.g. Change in validated scale score from baseline to week 8]
secondary: [Secondary outcome 2, e.g. Quality of life score]

results.primary_outcome_result: [Treatment group showed X% improvement vs Y% in placebo group. Statistical significance: p=[value]. Effect size (Cohen's d): [value]. Confidence interval: [95% CI: lower–upper].]
results.secondary_outcome_results: [Secondary outcomes: Biomarker Z decreased by X% in treatment group vs Y% in placebo (p=[value]). Quality of life score: no significant difference between groups (p=[value]).]
results.statistical_significance: Primary endpoint: p<0.05 / p=0.xxx. Note: multiple secondary endpoints should be interpreted with caution due to lack of multiple comparison correction.

adverse_events: Adverse events were mild and transient. Treatment group: [N] reported GI discomfort (n=[X], [X]%), headache (n=[X], [X]%). Placebo group: GI discomfort (n=[X], [X]%). No serious adverse events were reported. No withdrawals due to adverse events.
withdrawals: [N] participants withdrew from treatment group ([reason]), [N] from placebo ([reason]). Analysed by intention-to-treat.
limitations: Short duration (8 weeks) — no long-term data
Extract preparation not commercially available — limits real-world applicability
Primary outcome measure is subjective / validated scale — note if not objective biomarker
funding_source: [Funding body — note if industry funded or independent]
author_conclusion: The authors concluded that [species] extract at [dose] for [duration] produced statistically significant improvements in [primary outcome] compared to placebo, with a favorable safety profile in the study population. The authors call for larger, longer-term trials to confirm these findings.
bms_archive_interpretation: This RCT provides Level B evidence for [specific indication] in [specific population] using [specific extract and dose]. The finding should not be generalized to other preparations, doses, or populations without replication. Safety data from this study are limited by short duration and small sample size.
qa_notes: SEND TO scientific-qa-evidence-agent BEFORE USE. Verify: (1) PMID corresponds to this exact study. (2) Dose and extract match what is claimed in data.json. (3) Level B is appropriate — confirm RCT design is adequate. (4) Safety data is insufficient for long-term claims. (5) Confirm authors' conclusion language is not overstated in data.json narrative.
full_text_reason: Adverse event data and complete statistical methods require full text for verification. Abstract alone is insufficient for safety claims.
